events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer raw JSON. Need parse scenario. We are a tradesperson/inspector in manufacturing floor, torque & threaded fastening, in-progress. Crew proposes shortcut for Torque & threaded fastening, but submittal omits product listing page and project note that would decide whether shortcut allowed. Reference material: OEM/engineering specs.

Need decide practical compliant course. Shortcut not allowed without approval/documents. Decision likely needs_more_info or fail? Since work in-progress and crew proposes shortcut but submittal missing info that would decide whether shortcut allowed. Best course: stop/hold work until missing submittal info obtained and engineering/OEM confirms. Does that mean decision needs_more_info? The instructions say if work is dangerous or non-compliant, decision must be fail. Here shortcut not yet performed? Proposes. It might be non-compliant to use shortcut without approved documentation. Since missing submittal means cannot verify compliance; should not proceed. Decision could be fail because shortcut is not substantiated by submittal/OEM. But the scenario asks evaluate field condition and return appropriate decision. Since in-progress actively, but no component condition flagged. The shortcut is a deviation lacking documentation. A competent inspector would reject/fail the proposed shortcut until documentation provided. Could be needs_more_info if not yet executed. However if crew already executing shortcut? Proposes. Maybe not yet implemented. Since submittal omits product listing page/project note, can't decide; gather info. But task says choose practical compliant course. The compliant course: don't allow shortcut; require submittal. We can set decision fail for the proposed shortcut, with actions: stop/hold shortcut, obtain missing product listing page and project note, verify against OEM/engineering specs, get approval from engineer/QA, retorque/rework if shortcut already applied. Risk medium/high? Threaded fastening torque errors can cause structural/joint failure; missing approval high maybe. But since not yet executed maybe medium. Let's think.

We need output exactly JSON with fields. Some irrelevant fields: value 0, sound_source "", event_time 0, rate 0, order [], workable [] because not asked. percent_complete maybe 50? In-progress torque fastening. component_conditions empty list. confidence 0.9.

Findings: submittal incomplete; shortcut lacks product listing page and project note; cannot verify compliance with OEM/engineering specs; unapproved deviation from standard work.

Actions: halt proposed shortcut; withhold approval; request missing submittal pages and project note; review against OEM/engineering specs and approved procedures; obtain written engineering/QA approval before proceeding; if shortcut already used, inspect/rework/re-torque per spec.

Rationale: A torque/fastening shortcut cannot be used without the documentation that proves it is allowed; absent product listing and project note, compliance cannot be verified, so work must stop/pending approval to prevent non-compliant joints.

References: OEM/engineering specs; standard work / quality gate requirements; perhaps ASME/ISO? For threaded fasteners: ASME B18, ISO 898-1, SAE, torque spec. But reference material only OEM/engineering specs. We can list "OEM/engineering specifications" and "approved submittal/project documentation" and maybe "quality gate / standard work procedure". Keep concise.

Need ensure JSON valid, no code fence.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": [],
  "percent_complete": 50,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.9,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Proposed torque/threaded-fastening shortcut lacks required product listing page and project note in the submittal.",
    "Cannot verify that the shortcut is permitted for this product or project.",
    "Unapproved deviation from OEM/engineering torque specifications and standard work.",
    "Risk of incorrect clamp load, loose fasteners, or joint failure if shortcut is used."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop the proposed shortcut immediately and hold related fastening work.",
    "Request the missing product listing page and project note from the submittal.",
    "Verify the shortcut against OEM/engineering specs and approved project procedures.",
    "Obtain written engineering/QA approval before any shortcut is implemented.",
    "If any fasteners were already installed using the shortcut, inspect, remove, and re-torque/rework per spec."
  ],
  "rationale": "A torque or threaded-fastening shortcut cannot be accepted when the documentation needed to authorize it is missing. Without the product listing page and project note, compliance with OEM/engineering specs cannot be confirmed, so the work fails the quality gate and must stop pending approved documentation.",
  "references": [
    "OEM/engineering torque and fastening specifications",
    "Approved submittal and project note requirements",
    "Standard work / quality gate procedure for threaded fastening"
  ]
}
